title: Senecillis euryphylla
taxonomy: Senecillis euryphylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Madagascar and is a type of shrub or small tree. The plant is often classified in the genus Secamone, but some sources may group it under Senecillis. The classification of Senecillis euryphylla may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the Apocynaceae family. Further research may be necessary to confirm its exact classification within the plant kingdom.
uses: Senecillis euryphylla is known for its medicinal properties in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. It is traditionally used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Additionally, it is believed to have antiviral properties, making it useful in treating viral infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Senecillis euryphylla for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Senecillis euryphylla is a plant that contains toxic compounds, and its side effects are not well documented. However, plants in the Senecio genus, to which Senecillis euryphylla belongs, are known to contain pyrrolizidine alkaloids (PA), which can be toxic to humans and animals.
